# R1 Hermes Shim — agent install guide

You are installing a Rabbit R1 channel into Hermes Agent. This is a Hermes
PLATFORM PLUGIN. Do NOT patch the Hermes source tree. Earlier versions did
(copying r1_shim.py into gateway/platforms/ and editing config.py/run.py/
authz_mixin.py); that broke on every `hermes update`. The plugin path needs
zero core edits and survives updates.

## How it works
- The repo is a plugin: plugin.yaml (kind: platform) + __init__.py (exports
  `register`) + adapter.py (R1ShimAdapter + `register(ctx)`).
- It registers a `r1_shim` platform via `ctx.register_platform()`. Hermes mints
  `Platform("r1_shim")` on demand (Platform._missing_), reads `allow_all_env`
  off the registry entry for authorization, and auto-enables the channel when
  `R1_SHIM_TOKEN` is set. No core files change.
- It runs an OpenClaw-compatible WebSocket server (default port 18790). The R1
  pairs via a QR (scripts/make_qr.py) and chats through the normal Hermes
  pipeline.

## Install steps
1. Install + enable the plugin (platform plugins are allow-listed):
   hermes plugins install iammatthias/r1-hermes-shim
   hermes plugins enable r1-shim
2. Configure ~/.hermes/.env (a fixed token keeps the pairing QR stable):
   R1_SHIM_TOKEN=<openssl rand -hex 32>
   R1_SHIM_PORT=18790            # avoid 18789 if an intern-server stub owns it
   R1_SHIM_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=true  # the R1 authenticates at the WS layer
3. Restart the gateway:
   hermes gateway restart   # or systemctl restart hermes-gateway
4. Verify:
   journalctl -u hermes-gateway | grep -i r1_shim | tail   # want "listening on ws://0.0.0.0:18790/"
   ss -ltnp | grep ':18790'                                  # want a LISTEN line
5. Pairing QR:
   R1_SHIM_TOKEN=<token> R1_SHIM_PORT=18790 python3 scripts/make_qr.py
   # scan r1-pairing-qr.png in R1 -> Settings -> OpenClaw

## Migrating off the old patch-based install
If a previous install patched the Hermes source, revert it first so `hermes
update` runs clean, then install the plugin:
  HERMES_SRC=/usr/local/lib/hermes-agent   # or ~/.hermes/hermes-agent
  git -C "$HERMES_SRC" checkout -- gateway/config.py gateway/run.py gateway/authz_mixin.py
  rm -f "$HERMES_SRC/gateway/platforms/r1_shim.py"
Paired-device state in ~/.hermes/r1_shim/ is preserved across the migration.

## Optional env
R1_SHIM_AUTO_APPROVE (default true), R1_SHIM_TTS_VOICE, R1_SHIM_TTS_FORMAT
(mp3|pcm), R1_SHIM_UNIFIED_CHAT_ID + R1_SHIM_UNIFIED_PLATFORM (share one
session with another channel).
